text: I feel a strange liberation—being 'out' is both physical and existential. Outside the glass, separated from the meeting, unnoticed and undefined, I become an observer instead of a participant. In the second moment, the world seems to invite curiosity: displays of research and development, an air of innovation, and the persistent sense of moving beyond boundaries—outward, away from familiar enclosures. There's uncertainty, but also a quiet thrill, a readiness to see what emerges next in this state of being 'out.'
